name: Blueberry and Lemon Crumb Bars
recipeIngredient: 1/2 cup Cold butter, cut into 1/4-inch slices
recipeInstructions:
Preheat oven to 400°F / 200°C. Butter is unsalted unless otherwise stated. Lightly butter a 13- x 9-inch baking pan.
1. To make the crust, combine flour, sugar, lemon zest, and salt in the bowl of a food processor. With the motor running, add small pieces of cold butter at a time.
2. In a small cup, stir together the Egg yolk and vanilla extract.
3. With the motor running, gradually add the egg yolk mixture to the flour mixture. Process until the mixture comes together.
4. If the dough seems dry, add some of the cold water. Turn the dough out onto the prepared pan; press into an even layer over the bottom.
5. To make the filling, in a large bowl stir together sugar, flour, and nutmeg until blended. Add blueberries; stir to coat evenly. Spread over crust.
6. To make topping, work butter and brown sugar with a wooden spoon until combined. Gradually add flour, stirring until mixture resembles coarse crumbs. Sprinkle evenly over the blueberries.
7. Bake for 15 minutes. Reduce oven temperature to 350°F, then bake an additional 25-30 minutes, or until edges and topping are golden and blueberries are cooked through.
8. Cool on a wire rack before cutting into bars. Lightly sprinkle with powdered sugar before serving.
